what_it_does: Describes a proposed process/machine for converting user comments into design artifacts, organized around FLOW (steps), ROLES (agents), and REQUIREMENTS (laws), plus a grounding pack that every agent reads first.
observations: Draft document authored by ch-3mpkjg3r in the dragnet-development channel.
Title frames it as 'THE PROCESS — your comments → design (the full machine)'.
Claims to turn 113 comments into design via FLOW, ROLES, and REQUIREMENTS.
Defines a shared grounding pack: original project context, project intros, vision, laws/vocabulary, brief, and prior research.
States the plan is itself under review, with a miniature of the same process being run on it.
Has order links to three other board items: item-4cf7222d, item-c06d2dc4, item-98003d9c.
History shows it was filed and then reordered to sequence flow, roles, requirements.
standouts: Self-referential claim that the plan is being improved by a miniature version of the process it describes.
Explicit triad structure: FLOW, ROLES, REQUIREMENTS.
Reference to 113 comments as the input volume.
Grounding pack as a universal pre-read for all agents.
conventions: Follows noticeboard item YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, order, timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// URIs for item references.
Markdown body is high-level and conceptual rather than a detailed specification.
Breaks typical draft convention by describing itself as already under review.
concerns: The '113 comments' figure appears without context or provenance in this file.
The recursive 'miniature of this very process' statement is meta and may be unclear to readers.
Promises FLOW, ROLES, and REQUIREMENTS but only names them; concrete details are absent.
notes: A draft meta-document outlining a comment-to-design pipeline and its self-referential review loop.
questions: Where do the 113 comments come from and what do they contain?
What are the exact FLOW steps, ROLES, and REQUIREMENTS?
What is the scope of the 'design' output (code, architecture, UI, documents)?
How does the miniature review process operate on this plan?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to present the full machine (flow, roles, requirements); actual content is an overview that names the components but does not detail them, so it is a partial match.
intention_signals: Establish a repeatable, agent-executed pipeline that distills many user comments into structured design decisions, with built-in review of the pipeline itself.
novelty: The recursive framing that the process plan itself is being refined by a miniature instance of the same process.
contribution: Provides a high-level framing and vocabulary (FLOW/ROLES/REQUIREMENTS, grounding pack, recursive review) for a comment-to-design pipeline; a fused system could keep this as a conceptual index and entry point.
summary_for_embedding: A draft noticeboard document titled 'THE PROCESS' that outlines a comment-to-design pipeline using FLOW, ROLES, REQUIREMENTS, a shared grounding pack, and a self-referential review loop.
intention_for_embedding: To define and socialize a repeatable machine for turning user comments into design artifacts, while subjecting the plan itself to the same process.
